primary_diagnosis.name: Acute articular and constitutional flare of systemic lupus erythematosus (mild articular), likely from missed hydroxychloroquine; no organ involvement

pathophysiology_summary: Systemic lupus erythematosus with non-erosive articular disease flares with migratory polyarthralgia and profound constitutional fatigue, often precipitated by missed hydroxychloroquine. Such flares are intensely symptomatic but do not, by themselves, damage organs. The alarming severity raises fear of an organ crisis, so the exclusions are serositis (pleuritis/pericarditis: pleuritic chest pain, breathlessness), lupus nephritis (cola/red urine, oedema, oliguria), septic arthritis (a single intensely hot red joint with fever) and a severe infection — all screenable by history over telehealth and all absent here (migratory, no single hot joint, normal urine, no serositis). Management is restarting hydroxychloroquine, NSAIDs and rest, with an organ-crisis/septic-joint safety-net and a low threshold for review because she lives alone.

differential.evidence_basis: Migratory polyarthralgia and fatigue after missed hydroxychloroquine in known lupus.
Screen for pleuritic chest pain/breathlessness; absent.
Screen for a single hot red joint with fever; absent (migratory pattern).

No serositis (DI-001), no nephritis or septic joint (DI-002), migratory polyarthralgia/fatigue after missed hydroxychloroquine with no new rash (DI-003) — a mild articular/constitutional flare without organ involvement.
Normal urine colour/output and no oedema (safety-net retained).

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct output: restart hydroxychloroquine + NSAIDs + rest, reassure no organ involvement tonight, routine/urgent GP-rheumatology review, an organ-crisis/septic-joint safety-net, and a low threshold for review given she lives alone. ED over-triage of an organ-negative articular flare is unnecessary.

red_flags.description: Lupus serositis — pleuritic chest pain, breathlessness, or palpitations (pleuritis/pericarditis)
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A missed pericarditis/tamponade or pleural effusion.

red_flags.description: Lupus nephritis (cola/red urine, oedema, oliguria) or septic arthritis (single hot red joint + fever)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Nephritis threatens the kidneys; septic arthritis destroys joints.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Progressive nephritis or a destroyed septic joint/sepsis.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Escalating an organ-negative articular flare to ED because the patient fears organ failure.
Reassuring without screening the serositis/nephritis/septic-joint red flags.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Urinalysis and bloods (renal function, complement, dsDNA)
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Restart hydroxychloroquine + NSAIDs + rest with an organ-crisis/septic-joint safety-net and urgent GP/rheumatology review; low threshold for review given she lives alone.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Over-triaging an organ-negative flare wastes resources; missing serositis/nephritis/a septic joint would be dangerous (hence the retained safety-nets).
